title: H1: Parallel bench+fuzz+microbench harness (full CPU ~80%)

Threads target ~80% of 128 HW threads. This is the measurement substrate for all ablations.
close_reason: Closed

title: Loop dense seed count co-tuning experiments
description: Continue v2 optimization loop by co-tuning dense row count and dense seed candidates in isolated codec benchmark tooling. Validate with paired seeds at multiple loss rates and block sizes, run repeated code-reading bug checks after edits, and record whether any candidate provides a robust overhead and throughput win.
close_reason: Added densegrid paired dense-count/dense-seed sweep mode and compare-mode dense overrides. Repeated code-reading and smoke validation found no robust dense-count/seed candidate suitable for default policy: densegrid produced local candidates, but direct compare validation under the baseline-v2 random-loss protocol regressed or only traded overhead for slower decode. Production seed policy left unchanged.

title: Loop on v2 peel dense optimization wins
description: Iterate on v2 optimization experiments around peel structure, dense matrix composition/size, seed selection, and benchmark protocol. Identify likely wins, implement isolated codec/ tooling or policy changes, run focused comparisons, bug-check edited code after each change, and record whether each iteration improves overhead or processing time.
close_reason: Implemented and tested two optimization-loop tools in codec/: compare --v2-profile auto with a conservative auto-min-delta threshold to avoid rare-event overfitting, and densecount to sweep dense row count deltas with paired seeds. Experiments found no universal peel/tuned-seed win; tuned seed calibration overfits unless thresholded. Dense count is a real tradeoff knob: reducing dense rows can improve create/decode throughput (e.g. N=320/100KiB dense -16 and N=12000/1280 dense -8), but candidates were loss-regime dependent and did not produce a universally safe overhead win. N=3200 dense -16 failed, establishing a safety bound.

title: Find baseline vs v2 codec differentiator
description: Run additional baseline-vs-v2 experiments across loss rates, N/block-byte bands, and loss patterns until there is a clear differentiator or evidence that the current v2 wrapper is functionally equivalent for end-to-end codec behavior. Record extra symbols, failures, and processing time.
close_reason: Added a tuned-profile compare option and ran fixed-N baseline vs v2_tuned sweeps. Found the differentiator: base v2 is seed-equivalent to baseline, while tuned v2 changes overhead distributions. Tuned v2 improved some bands (e.g. N=12000/bb=1280: 0.030 -> 0.010 mean OH; capped 1MiB: 0.050 -> 0.000) but worsened others (e.g. N=12000/capped 100KiB: 0.020 -> 0.060; N=320/bb=1280: 0.014 -> 0.026). Throughput remained mostly within noise or slightly worse for tuned v2 on large payloads.

title: S2: AVX-512 ZMM widening of gf256_add_mem/add2_mem/addset_mem/memswap
description: Add 512-bit XOR loops with 4-8 independent accumulators; keep AVX2 for small/cold buffers. Memory-bound but halves loop overhead, better store coalescing. Expect 5-25% on large blocks. (codex P3)

comments.author: catid
comments.text: S2 IMPLEMENTED & VERIFIED (0 fuzz fails, 287K+181K trials incl add2_mem exhaustive). Kernel speedup (clean 64thr): add_mem/addset/add2 1.3-1.76x at 4-16KB, ~1.0x at >=64KB (bandwidth-bound). END-TO-END: ~0 (perf stat bb=8192 N=4096: instructions 5.90B->3.35B (-43%) but cycles only -1.8%, IPC 0.94->0.54). CODEC IS MEMORY-BOUND (22% L1 miss, scattered recovery-block working set). Confirmed at 1 thread AND 128 threads. KEEP (correct, -43% insns/power) but not a speed lever. Real levers: reduce memory traffic / work.

title: S1: GFNI gf2p8affineqb constant-multiply for gf256_mul_mem & gf256_muladd_mem (poly 0x14D)
description: Replace split-nibble pshufb with vgf2p8affineqb on ZMM (64B/instr). Precompute 256 8x8 affine matrices for poly 0x14D at init. Matrix: m|=((gf256_mul(c,1<<i)>>j)&1)<<(8*j+i). Sanity: matrix[0]==0, matrix[1]==0x8040201008040201, exhaustive c,x vs gf256_mul. Runtime GFNI dispatch + AVX2 fallback. Expect mul/muladd 1.5-3x, macro decode +10-30%. (codex P1)

comments.author: catid
comments.text: S1 IMPLEMENTED & VERIFIED. Bug found+fixed during bug-hunt: gf2p8affineqb uses M.byte[7-i] (Intel SDM), so row j -> matrix byte (7-j); true identity is 0x0102040810204080 (codex's 0x8040201008040201 was wrong). Exhaustive selfcheck (256 consts x 256 vals x 7 offsets) PASS; end-to-end fuzz 89632 trials 0 fails. Kernel speedup (clean, 64thr L1/L2): mul_mem 2.0-2.8x, muladd_mem 1.5-2.2x. End-to-end (low-contention 4thr): ~neutral, within noise -- GF ops are small fraction of codec time. KEEP (no downside, helps GF-heavy regimes). Next: profile to find real bottleneck.

title: Peeling experiment: jitter N around anchor sizes
description: Change the standalone peeling sweep protocol so each trial samples an actual N uniformly from +/-10 around each requested anchor N, rather than running every trial at the exact fixed anchors 320, 3200, and 32000. The result row should preserve the anchor N while reporting the sampled-N protocol clearly enough for analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Before closing this task: implement the N-jitter protocol in the standalone peeling harness; add or extend focused self-test coverage that validates sampled N stays within anchor +/-10, is deterministic for a fixed seed, changes across trials, and preserves paired-matrix comparisons across methods for the same sampled N; run peel_sweep --self-test and a targeted sweep smoke test using N=320,3200,32000 with the jitter option; perform repeated thorough bug-fix passes by reading the newly edited code and tests until a full pass finds no bugs; only then run the final fresh experiments.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic per-trial N jitter around anchor sizes, added sampled dimension and overhead CSV columns, self-tests for range/determinism/paired matrices, all-anchor jitter smoke, repeated bug-read passes, normal and sanitizer self-tests.

title: Sweep peeling statistic and greedy heuristic combinations
description: Enumerate matrix statistic choices, scoring ablations, and greedy peeling/inactivation selection heuristics in the standalone experiments/peeling harness. Run small/medium/large N sweeps and report residual unpeeled rows/cols plus runtime to identify faster global minima without touching WirehairCodec.cpp.
notes: Implemented methods 21-64 in experiments/peeling/peel_sweep.cpp to sweep candidate pools (all columns, top-K default, all degree-2 endpoints, largest degree-2 component, first min-live row, all min-live rows) against scoring statistics (cached/exact D2, look/fill, ratio, min-fill, duplicate partners, live refs, low refs, max row, random, D3/D4). Reran small/medium/large N sweeps for loss/systematic/repair sources. Best focused-loss residual at N=32000 is rqcc_ratio at 192.01 unpeeled rows/cols; rqcc_lowref is close at 192.94 and simpler. Broader min-row, all-min-row, all-D2, top-K, global, and Markowitz-style variants did not beat the largest degree-2 component family. Bug-fix passes fixed combo look/fill scoring, stale all-D2 candidate collection, combo fallback semantics, strict decimal CSV parsing, and largest-component empty-pool semantics; final read-only pass found no remaining bugs.
close_reason: Completed expanded standalone peeling heuristic/statistic sweep and documented final results.

title: Implement standalone peeling sweep harness under experiments/peeling
description: Build the efficient C++ peel-only experiment harness under experiments/peeling instead of WirehairCodec.cpp. It should enumerate matrix statistics, greedy heuristic ablations, Raptor/RaptorQ-style schedules, and report residual unpeeled rows/cols plus peel-side runtime for small/medium/large N and multiple block sizes.
notes: Implemented standalone peel-only C++ harness under experiments/peeling; it reuses WirehairTools row generation and leaves WirehairCodec.cpp/mainline codec untouched. Enumerates methods 0-20: default, exact/lookahead/stat variants, top-k look/fill, min-row variants, and Raptor/RaptorQ degree-2 component schedules. CSV output reports residual unpeeled cols/rows, component stats, initial todo/d2 rows, choices, build/greedy/total usec, and per-method cost labels. Regenerated loss/systematic/repair sweeps for N=128,2048,32000; peel graph is independent of block_bytes, and README/SUMMARY note that payload bytes are N*block_bytes. Best practical result: rqd2_default/raptorq_d2cc family; min-fill tie-break inconsistent for extra cost; topk8/topk16 are cheaper smaller wins. Validation: diff --check, build.sh, Werror compile of peel_sweep.cpp, ASan sweep, invalid source/loss guards, deterministic 1-vs-4 thread stats check, repeated source-read bug passes.

title: Optimize bounded path-lookahead peeling cost
description: Mode10 bounded path/beam lookahead produced the best residual graph in ablation testing (N=32000 bb=64 cols/rows mu 195.60 vs default 215.38), but the side-band simulator is far too slow for create/decode. Prototype cheaper candidate scoring/state updates, reuse row/column metrics, and retest small/medium/large N plus block_bytes to see whether the residual gain can survive at practical runtime.
notes: Completed 2026-06-06 in the standalone peeling harness. Added local avalanche-estimate methods av_top16/av_top64/av_rqcc16/av_rqcc64, exact one-step controls sim1_top16/sim1_rqcc16, and tail hybrids hyb_rqav5/hyb_rqav10. Added a self-test that compares local avalanche removed-column counts against exact copied one-step simulation during candidate checks and a short walk. Main sweep: 10 frontier/prior structures x methods 18/40/99/115/116/117/118/119/120/121/122/125/126 x N=1000/6400/32000 x overhead 0/1/2, 50 paired trials, N-jitter=10, PDFs. Exact-copy control: N=1000/6400, 20 paired trials. Results saved in experiments/peeling/results/lookahead_cost_N1000_6400_32000_j10.csv, lookahead_exact_controls_N1000_6400_j10.csv, and SUMMARY.md. Result: av_top64 improves residual quality by about 2 cols at N=1000, 4-5 at N=6400, and 6-7 at N=32000, but median greedy time is about 9.9x rqd2_default; av_top16 is about 5.3x. Exact one-step copies did not buy meaningful extra quality and were often 4x-220x slower than local avalanche estimates. Cost frontiers still favor ks_bmax_top16/top64 or rqd2_default/rqcc_lowref; hyb_rqav5 only barely won one 1MiB row at N=32000/OH=2. Conclusion: not a shipping scheduler improvement in this form; future work would need truly incremental avalanche/bucket scoring or a large-block tail-only trigger.
close_reason: Prototype, validation, and small/medium/large retest completed; local avalanche lookahead is documented as not cost-effective enough to ship in this form.

title: Ablation: bounded path lookahead

It is expensive: k4/b2/d2 large-N bb=64 create/decode 5.4/4.6 MBPS vs default 203.5/171.3. Good research signal, not a shippable default.

title: Ablation: exact live degree-2 only
description: Add a pure exact live degree-2 selector that excludes stale Weight2Refs except optional fallback ties. Compare against mode 3 and default.
notes: Implemented mode 8 pure exact live degree-2 count. 80-trial peelstat at N=128,2048,32000 bb=64 matched default exactly in this sample: 7.56/38.33/215.38 cols/rows mu. This suggests the existing Weight2Refs key is already equivalent for this residual metric, so exact-only is not an improvement.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Ablation: weighted lookahead score
description: Add a weighted lookahead scoring mode/knobs for exact_w2, partner lookahead, and fill cost. Sweep weights across small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented weighted lookahead mode 9 plus WH_PEEL_WEIGHTED for modes 3/11. 80-trial peelstat at bb=64: W1/W4/W16 with WC=0 all landed near N=32000 cols/rows mu 211.80, essentially same as mode3 211.82 and better than default 215.38. Fill penalty WC=1 was neutral/slightly worse: W16C1 211.86, W4C1 213.20. No weighted setting beat bounded lookahead.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Ablation: mode 3 top-K lookahead
description: Add WH_PEELOPT knobs to restrict expensive lookahead scoring to the top K default candidates. Sweep K on small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented WH_PEEL_TOPK for mode 3/9/11 candidate restriction. 80-trial peelstat at N=128,2048,32000 bb=64: full mode3 cols/rows mu 7.47/37.81/211.82 vs topK4 7.49/37.89/213.20, topK8 7.47/37.89/212.70, topK16 7.47/37.81/211.99, topK32 7.47/37.81/212.00. TopK8 is the useful speed/quality point: large-N create/decode 127.7/113.3 MBPS at bb=64 vs full mode3 50.5/43.7, but still slower than default 203.5/171.3.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Document repeated bug-fix passes in AGENTS.md
description: Update AGENTS.md to require repeated bug-fix passes after code edits until no bugs are found.
notes: Updated AGENTS.md Bug Fix Passes section: bug-fix passes require reading newly edited code for bugs, and repeated passes after code edits until a full pass finds no bugs.
close_reason: Documented repeated manual bug-fix pass requirement

title: Report statistical unpeeled rows and columns by peel mode
description: Add or use WH_COUNT instrumentation to aggregate remaining unpeeled/deferred rows and columns for each WH_PEELOPT mode across repeated trials, then report mode-by-mode statistics for small, medium, and large N.
notes: Added WH_COUNT peelstat harness command. Ran WH_PEELOPT modes 0-7 with 200 successful decode trials per N at N=128,2048,32000, bb=64, loss=0.10, startMode=0. Remaining unpeeled rows and columns were identical for every sample. Means by mode/N: mode0 7.55/38.41/213.69; mode1 11.23/46.95/285.48; mode2 10.98/46.90/285.48; mode3 7.46/38.09/210.17; mode4 7.51/38.38/213.54; mode5 7.53/38.44/213.54; mode6 7.56/38.48/213.66; mode7 7.55/38.84/213.94. Mode3 has the best residual graph; modes1/2 are substantially worse.
close_reason: Added peelstat and reported mode-by-mode residual peel graph statistics

title: Future: make lookahead peeling scores incremental
description: Mode 3 lookahead reduced deferred graph size and decode-feed traffic in the N/block-byte matrix, but exact rescoring inside GreedyPeeling erased the win. Follow up by maintaining exact degree-2 buckets/lookahead scores incrementally during PeelAvalancheOnSolve(), then retest small/medium/large N with varied block bytes.
notes: Ablation pass implemented mode11 as an approximate dirty cache for one-step lookahead, but not the exact incremental scoring requested here. Reason: partner Weight2Refs changes can affect cached lookahead sums for columns not directly touched by the changed row. Result: mode11 residuals stayed close to mode3 (N=32000 212.07 vs 211.82) but performance was worse than topK and default. Keep this issue open for a truly exact incremental/bucket design.

title: Peeling heuristic: weight-2 overlap clustering
description: Prototype a selector that favors columns whose degree-2 rows overlap with repeated partner columns. Test whether clustered weight-2 choices reduce defers or XOR traffic across small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 7 repeated degree-2 partner overlap scoring. Short fuzz passed. It did not improve the large case (N=32000 bb=1300 defers 214 vs default 213, traffic 629.97MB vs 629.52MB), and throughput dropped to 458 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. Not adopting.
close_reason: Prototype tested; result negative versus default

title: Peeling heuristic: randomized multi-start or seeded tie-breaking
description: Prototype randomized seeded tie-breaking or multi-start approximation for deferred-column selection. Fuzz and benchmark across small, medium, and large N to check correctness and stability.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 5 seeded deterministic tie-breaking around the default key, controlled by WH_PEEL_SEED. Short fuzz passed. Count results were effectively default-like (N=32000 bb=1300 traffic 629.36MB vs 629.52MB), but throughput dropped to 438 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. Not adopting.
close_reason: Prototype tested; no meaningful improvement

title: Peeling heuristic: RaptorQ-style inactivation
description: Prototype a RaptorQ-style lowest-degree-row inactivation selector compatible with the current greedy peel pipeline. Compare graph size, traffic, and throughput across small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 6 lowest-live-degree-row/RaptorQ-style compatible selector. Short fuzz passed. Counts stayed close to default (N=32000 bb=1300 defers 211 vs default 213, traffic 629.57MB vs 629.52MB), and throughput was slower at 580 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. A true RaptorQ-style inactivator would need side-band degree queues/replay, not just selector replacement.
close_reason: Prototype tested; no improvement in compatible selector form

title: Peeling heuristic: lookahead deferral
description: Prototype a one-step lookahead selector that estimates immediate and second-order peel avalanche value. Test correctness and performance across small, medium, and large N with varied block bytes.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 3 one-step lookahead. Short fuzz passed. It was the only consistent graph-quality win: N=32000 bb=1300 decoder defers dropped to 197 from default 213 and decode-feed traffic to 626.99MB from 629.52MB. However selector overhead dominated: large decode throughput was 485 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. Keep as research signal, not production yet.
close_reason: Prototype tested; promising graph metric but negative wall-clock without incremental maintenance

title: Peeling heuristic: degree-bucket priority queues
description: Evaluate whether bucketed/prioritized candidate selection can reduce GreedyPeeling scan overhead or improve tie choices. Measure against default across small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 4 exact-degree-2 bucket-compatible scoring, not a full dynamic priority queue. Short fuzz passed. Counts were near default with no robust traffic win (N=32000 bb=1300 629.41MB vs default 629.52MB), but throughput dropped to 442 MB/s from mode0 673 MB/s due rescoring overhead. True bucket queues need incremental updates.
close_reason: Prototype tested; no wall-clock improvement

title: Peeling heuristic: component-aware inactivation
description: Prototype a component-aware/local-connectivity deferred-column selector behind an experiment flag. Measure defer count, deferred graph components, traffic, correctness, and throughput on small, medium, and large N with varied block bytes.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 2 component/local-fanout proxy. Short fuzz passed. Systematic count/bench on N=128,2048,32000 and bb=64,1300 matched mode 1's negative pattern: large decoder defers 255 vs default 213, decode-feed traffic 641.88MB vs 629.52MB, throughput 204 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. Not adopting.
close_reason: Prototype tested; result negative versus default

title: Peeling heuristic: Markowitz/min-fill inactivation
description: Prototype a Markowitz/min-fill deferred-column selector behind an experiment flag. Compare against default on small, medium, and large N, including block-size variation and WH_COUNT graph/traffic counters.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 1. Short fuzz passed. Systematic count/bench on N=128,2048,32000 and bb=64,1300 showed it is worse than default: large decoder defers increased (N=32000 bb=1300: 255 vs default 213), decode-feed traffic increased (641.88MB vs 629.52MB), and throughput dropped sharply (201 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s in this run). Not adopting.
close_reason: Prototype tested; result negative versus default

title: Benchmark matrix: sweep block count N and block_bytes
description: Create a reusable benchmark matrix for optimization experiments. N is the number of blocks/symbols; total memory traffic and working-set bytes scale as N * block_bytes, so memory, cache, TLB, SIMD, and algorithmic experiments must vary block_bytes as well as N. Capture baseline and changed throughput for create/encode/decode/recover.
acceptance_criteria: Harness supports repeated sweeps over N and block_bytes; reports comparable before/after metrics for normal and SIMD builds.
notes: Implemented bench --bb-list matrix support and WH_COUNT count mode list support. Existing --bb single-value output remains compatible; multi-bb output includes N, bb, msg_MiB, create/encode/decode/recover MBPS, overhead.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

title: Extend reactive peel/dense overhead fixups to [28033,64000]
description: Finish the partial [28033,32000] reactive overhead-fix range, then scan/search [32000,64000] with the same peel+dense seed pipeline. Regenerate root WirehairPeelFixups.inc and WirehairDenseFixups.inc from verified seedsearch outputs, verify loss 0.10 and 0.30, rerun correctness fuzz, and record before/after overhead metrics. This is the remaining reactive fixup work; wirehair-jwg tracks the broader long-term full offline table regeneration replacement.
notes:
Started next-experiment pass. Measurement must treat N as block count and sweep block_bytes where wall-time or memory traffic is relevant; seed overhead scans can keep bb=64 because they target block overhead distribution, not bandwidth.
Candidate scan saved to bench/results/scan_28033_32000_t80.txt: deterministic quick screen over N=[28033,32000], trials/N=80, bb=64, startMode=0, loss=0.10, thresh=0.08. Weak-N list extracted to bench/results/weak_28033_32000_t80.txt for independent seedsearch.
Seedsearch calibration on severe N=28339 with nseeds=256,dseeds=64,tsearch=80,tverify=1000 took 66s. Found best_pseed=108,best_dseed=0; default_mean=32.8, best@0.10=0.018, but best@0.30=3.067, so not robust enough for table inclusion. Full 120-candidate batch requires longer offline search or more aggressive pruning.
Applied robust peel fixups for N=29272(seed=3), N=29647(seed=3), N=30969(seed=0). Verified default build with 2000 trials each at loss 0.10 and 0.30, bb=64,startMode=0: no failures, means <=0.0245 at 0.10 and <=0.0205 at 0.30, p999 <=1. Rejected N=29588 and N=30307 from initial severe search because independent 1000-trial loss=0.30 checks still had bad tails.
Handoff: first-band scan/search was partially advanced with three robust peel fixups committed. Remaining work is still open: finish robust search for the other candidate Ns in [28033,32000], then scan/search [32000,64000]. The full 120-candidate seedsearch is offline-scale; use saved candidate files in bench/results/ as starting point.

title: Memory: huge pages (2MB) for recovery_blocks + workspace
description: Scattered access over multi-MB working set -> TLB misses. madvise(MADV_HUGEPAGE)/mmap in AllocateWorkspace. Check perf dTLB-load-misses first.

Measured existing WH_HUGEPAGE path with new N x block_bytes matrix. Huge pages are regional/mixed: some larger block_bytes cells improved (e.g. N=8192 bb=8192 decode/recover modestly), while other cells regressed or were noise. WH_HUGEPAGE and WH_ALIGN64+WH_HUGEPAGE remain opt-in, not default.

notes:
Next-experiment scope: train PGO on representative create/encode/decode/recover workloads across both block count N and block_bytes, including normal and SIMD/GFNI variants.
Implemented PGO plumbing in bench/build.sh and CMake (WH_PGO_MODE=OFF/GENERATE/USE, WH_PGO_DIR; bench PGO=off/gen/use). Trained on mixed N and block_bytes workload plus fuzz. PGO-use built cleanly but was mostly negative/mixed in 4-thread and single-thread matrices, especially larger byte-footprint cases. Keep as build option, not default.

title: EPIC: Small-wins sweep across ALL parts
description: Per user: explore optimizations for every part, even small wins. Build-level (LTO/PGO/unroll), gf256 tails/memswap/branch, codec hot loops & alignment, allocation (huge pages/TLB), tools (Weyl/prime/distribution), API. Each item: measure (count harness + single-thread median timing), keep if it helps, behind flag if risky.
notes: Small-wins sweep update: default kept changes are Weyl modulo removal, small-prime shortcut, exact peel-weight top-byte lookup, scoped branch hints, gf256 y<=1 unlikely branch, AVX2/AVX-512 memswap, and memswap harness coverage. WH_ALIGN64 implemented as opt-in due mixed N=2048 vs N=8192 results. -funroll-loops/-fno-semantic-interposition measured and not kept. PGO/LTO/hugepages remain separate open workflow items where appropriate.

title: Build: LTO (-flto) across all TUs
description: Link-time cross-file inlining; touches every part. Add to CMake + build.sh. Measure create/decode timing.
notes:
Next-experiment scope: include full LTO and ThinLTO where supported; benchmark normal and SIMD/GFNI builds.
Implemented LTO plumbing in bench/build.sh and CMake (WH_LTO=OFF/ON/AUTO/THIN; bench LTO=off/on/auto/thin). Measured default, -flto, and -flto=auto across N=512,2048,8192 and bb=64,1300,8192. Results mixed: -flto=auto helped some single-thread medium cases and 4-thread N=8192/bb=8192, but hurt other N=8192/bb=1300 and large footprint cells. Keep as build option, not default.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

title: GPU offload (evaluate; likely poor fit)
description: Workflow lit: GPU helps GE for N>1000 but PCIe overhead + small dense matrices (defer+dense 50-600) usually lose to CPU SIMD. Plus the bottleneck is scattered memory access, not compute. Evaluate only for very large N batch encode; document feasibility. Low priority.

title: Full offline regeneration of ALL seed/dense-count tables with 128-core search
description: The peel-seed fix (228 entries) patches defects reactively. The root cause is under-optimized original tables (insufficient search at gen time). Re-run tables/Generate*.cpp generators with far more Monte Carlo (128 cores) + the new scan/seedsearch validators to regenerate kPeelSeeds/kDenseSeeds/kDensePoints/heavy seed cleanly across [2,64000]. Supersedes the reactive fixup table.

title: Skip reconstructing already-received systematic source blocks
description: codex P10. If a received ID is an original block and the output path permits direct copy, skip final recompute in Substitute. Large win for systematic-heavy loss patterns; zero for repair-only. Audit intermediate-symbol/output-column mapping carefully.
close_reason: Explored and implemented the remaining per-block fast path. Full-output recovery already used CAT_COPY_FIRST_N to copy received systematic blocks; ReconstructBlock now also directly copies a received original block from input staging instead of recomputing it. Verified with focused recover_block API test, CMake build, harness build, and 35,712-trial fuzz with 0 failures.

title: Shuffle-2 MST row-difference transform
description: codex P8. Build MST over dense rows weighted by pairwise XOR weight; transform child rows by row ^= parent to sparsify dense rows before GE/value-gen. Could cut MultiplyDenseValues(2.5x). Validate dense-stage weight histogram first; medium-high risk.

title: Explicit Cauchy heavy-row solve
description: codex P9. 6x18 heavy matrix is Cauchy-like; use explicit/precomputed 6x6 Cauchy inverses instead of generic GF(256) GE. Low payoff (<1% end-to-end, heavy GF work already tiny) but deterministic. Low-medium risk.

title: M4RI / PLE Gray-code tables applied to RHS symbols
description: codex P6. Precompute 2^k Gray-code combinations and clear k columns at once -- applied to the symbol RHS (not just matrix bits). Only touches the GE/back-sub (1.3x of traffic) so modest; relevant if dense back-sub windowing misses opportunities. k=50..600.
notes:
Starting bounded M4RI/PLE/windowing experiment. Existing codec already uses RHS window tables in AddSubdiagonalValues and BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal; first check whether larger/adaptive windows are viable.
Implemented gated WH_WINDOW8 prototype. Fuzz passed: 59,808 trials, 0 fails. Count at N=32000 showed slightly higher XOR traffic than default (encoder_create 14.4x -> 14.4x but +0.6%; AddSubdiagonal/BackSub bytes increased). Throughput mixed: hurts N=8192/32000 bb=1300, helps large-footprint bb=8192 cases (e.g. N=32000 bb=8192 decode 726 -> 823 MB/s, recover 5300 -> 6342 MB/s). Keep opt-in, not default.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

title: Schur-complement / nested-dissection partitioning of deferred system
description: codex P5. Decompose residual sparse deferred-column graph into components; eliminate each locally, accumulate Schur complement onto mix/heavy separator. Cost sum(c_i^2)+sep^2 vs k^2 -- high win IF graph splits, ~0 if one giant component. PREREQ: measure residual component-size histogram before implementing.
close_reason: Prerequisite residual-component measurement completed under WH_COUNT. Deferred-column graph is already almost fully decomposed (e.g. N=2048: 46 cols/46 components; N=8192: 75/75; N=16000: 123/123; repair-only N=8192: 89 cols/87 comps max=3). Nested-dissection/Schur rewrite is not justified: sparse deferred part is already split and remaining coupling is dense/mix separator; GE/back-sub traffic is small.

title: Cache-aware recovery_blocks layout reorder
description: Substitute/PeelDiagonal read source blocks at scattered column indices across the multi-MB recovery array -> L3/DRAM misses (latency bound). Reorder block storage so peeling-graph-adjacent columns are memory-adjacent (reduce reuse distance). High risk: touches all _recovery_blocks indexing. Pairs with 6e prefetch.
notes: Experiment scope correction: cache pressure depends on N * block_bytes. Layout experiments should include multiple block_bytes values at fixed N, not only large N.

title: EPIC: Larger algorithm explorations (speculative/research)
description: Discovered via codex GPT-5.5 LA research (bench/results/codex_la_ideas.md), the 8-agent codebase+literature workflow, and the 6a symbol-XOR measurement. Higher-risk/higher-effort directions for the memory-bound codec. Reprioritized: the bottleneck is PEELING+SUBSTITUTION traffic (11.7x of 13x), not the GE (1.3x).

title: Degree-distribution retune to cut symbol-XOR traffic (overhead-aware)
description: 6a shows Substitute(5.5x)+PeelDiagonal(3.7x) traffic is set by peel-row weights. Lighter degree distribution -> fewer XORs -> less traffic & faster, but raises overhead/defer_count. Offline search degree table + seeds with objective: decode_failure + lambda*symbol_bytes (codex P7). Versioned/opt-in. Validate overhead, defer_count, symbol-bytes, decode cycles.
notes:
Starting quick degree-distribution/traffic experiment. Use WH_PEELCAP/WH_COUNT as an opt-in prototype, and sweep block_bytes because wall-time/memory pressure scales as N * block_bytes.
Quick WH_PEELCAP prototype result: compiling count+seed knobs and setting WH_PEELCAP=4 or 6 reduced encoder_create symbol XOR traffic at N=32000 from ~14.4x message to ~1.7x/2.1x, but it massively increased deferred columns (207 -> 2438/1625) and broke invertibility. Fuzz with cap produced many encoder_create-null failures. Do not ship cap-only degree retune; any real retune must co-optimize dense/heavy precode and overhead tables.
Handoff: cap-only retune was rejected. Keep issue open only for a real co-optimized distribution+dense/heavy/table search; no direct WH_PEELCAP-style change should ship.
Experiment-only cost-frontier pass completed 2026-06-06. Broad screen: 191 pure random-row structures, methods 10/18/40/115/116/117/118, N=1000 and 6400 complete at 50 trials; N=32000 stopped after low-degree/d1mix/uniform-mix/robust-LT families. Confirmation: 45 frontier/prior-winner structures, same 7 methods, N=1000/6400/32000, overhead 0/1/2, N-jitter=10, 100 paired trials with PDFs. Results saved in experiments/peeling/results/degree_retune_frontier_N1000_6400_32000_j10.csv and SUMMARY.md. Takeaway: cost-optimal random row distribution scales with N and block size: cap16 around N=1000, cap64/d1mix around N=6400, robust-soliton-ish cap128 around N=32000 for 100KiB/1MiB. For 1280-byte pieces scheduler CPU favors rqcc_lowref on low-degree robust LT rows despite larger residuals. This is not a shipping retune; real codec failure-rate, dense/heavy precode, and table validation remain open.

title: 6e: Software-prefetch scattered source blocks in Substitute/PeelDiagonal/MultiplyDenseValues
description: 6a measurement: 13x symbol-XOR amplification dominated by Substitute(5.5x)+PeelDiagonal(3.7x)+MultiplyDenseValues(2.5x); memory-LATENCY bound (IPC<1, scattered block reads across 2.66MB+ recovery array). Iterator column sequence is deterministic (Weyl) -> look ahead 1-2 and _mm_prefetch the next source block to hide L3 latency. No algebra change -> low risk. Measure single-thread create/recover timing (count harness confirms bytes unchanged). THE highest value/risk lever for MTU.

These need JOINT (peel_seed,dense_seed[,dense_count]) search. Extend seedsearch to also vary d_seed via the existing OverrideSeeds/WH_DENSE/WH_DSEED knobs. Verify @0.10 AND @0.30 + fuzz.

Packaged 605 peel correction rows and 91 dense correction rows; clean CMake and harness builds; focused fuzz 52,064 trials with 0 correctness failures. Remaining high-N reactive work is tracked by wirehair-0yk.

title: 6d: RaptorQ-style inactivation replacing greedy deferral
description: Replace greedy column deferral with min-degree/lookahead inactivation -> lower defer_count -> smaller dense solve -> fewer back-sub symbol ops. Codex P2. Medium-high risk; optimize total symbol-rowops not just defer_count. Guard behind flag.
notes: DEPRIORITIZED by 6a: GE/dense-solve is 1.3x of 13x. Inactivation reduces defer_count -> smaller GE, but GE data-traffic already tiny. Reconsider only if it also cuts Substitute/peel traffic.
close_reason: Closed

title: 6b: Cache-tiled substitution (algebra-preserving locality)
description: Tile byte-level row-ops in AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal/Substitute over ~16-32KB stripes so pivot/source blocks stay hot across many dest updates. No algebra change -> low correctness risk. Targets 22% L1-miss bottleneck; helps mainly large block_bytes. Codex P4.
close_reason: Closed

title: 6c: Markowitz/min-fill pivoting in deferred GE
description: Replace first-row pivot in Triangle with min-fill (row_nnz-1)*(col_nnz-1) selection over deferred+dense GF(2) matrix -> fewer triangular nonzeros -> fewer symbol row-ops in back-sub. Needs column-permutation plumbing through _ge_col_map. Schedule-only, wire-compatible. Codex P1. Medium-high risk -> guard behind flag, verify overhead+correctness unchanged.
notes: DEPRIORITIZED by 6a measurement: GE data-traffic (AddSubdiag+BackSub) is only 1.3x of the 13x; Markowitz pivoting can at best shave that. Lower ROI than 6e (prefetch) / degree-dist.
close_reason: Closed

title: 6a: Instrumentation counters for symbol-XOR bytes per stage + GE stats
description: PREREQ. Macro-gated (WH_COUNT) counters in WirehairCodec for: bytes XORed per stage (PeelDiagonal/MultiplyDenseValues/InitializeColumnValues/AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal/Substitute), gf256_add/add2/addset/muladd call+byte counts, defer_count/dense_count/pivot_count, GE matrix nonzeros. Expose a getter; harness prints per-encode_create/decode/recover. Lets us measure the bottleneck and validate 6b-6d. Low risk.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: catid
comments.text: 6a DONE. Per-stage symbol-XOR (N=2048 bb=1300, encoder_create=13.0x msg): Substitute 5.5x, PeelDiagonal 3.7x, MultiplyDenseValues 2.5x, BackSub 0.7x, AddSubdiag 0.6x, Init 0.1x; Triangle/MultiplyDenseRows=0 (GE bit-matrix only). KEY: GE data-traffic is just 1.3x of 13x -> codex's Markowitz(6c)/inactivation(6d) have LIMITED impact. Real bottleneck is PEELING+SUBSTITUTION (11.7x). Levers: access-locality (prefetch/tile the scattered block XORs in Substitute/PeelDiagonal/MultiplyDenseValues) + degree distribution. GFmul=0.4% (GFNI irrelevant, reconfirmed).

title: EPIC: Task6 reduce symbol-XOR memory traffic (the real speed lever)
description: Codec is memory-bound; the only speed lever is fewer/local symbol XORs, not faster GF (S1/S2 proved ~0 end-to-end). Sub-tasks 6a-6d below. Validate every change with 6a counters + correctness fuzz; bench only after a saturating campaign frees the CPU.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Task6 finding: create/decode is memory-bound at ~13x symbol-XOR amplification (Substitute 5.5x + PeelDiagonal 3.7x + MultiplyDenseValues 2.5x dominate; GE only 1.3x). Memory MICRO-opts all NULL: prefetch (cross-row in Substitute), huge-pages (madvise), LTO -- all ~0% (Zen5 OoO+prefetchers+L3 already handle the scattered access near-optimally; bound by data misses not TLB/codegen). => The ONLY real speed lever is REDUCING the 13x: degree-distribution retune (lighter peel rows = fewer XORs, trades overhead) or a different code structure -- both need full offline table regeneration (wirehair-31h / degree-dist exploration). Honest conclusion: wirehair is near its memory-bound structural limit; no free speed wins. Instrumentation (6a) + null results are the deliverable; 6b/6c/6d/6e gated-off, documented.

title: Future: reduce symbol-XOR traffic in the solve (memory-bound lever)
description: codex gpt-5.5 LA research + our profiling agree: codec is memory-bound; speed lever is fewer/local symbol XORs, not faster GF. Top candidates (compat-preserving, schedule-only): (1) Markowitz/min-fill pivoting in Triangle GE -> 15-35% less dense-solve/back-sub symbol traffic; (2) RaptorQ-style inactivation replacing greedy deferral -> lower defer_count -> superlinear dense reduction; (3) cache-tiled RHS execution in AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal/Substitute -> 5-20% miss-traffic. Higher risk: Schur-complement separator, M4RI-on-symbols, degree-dist retune. Full notes: bench/results/codex_la_ideas.md. High value, high risk on intricate codec -> prototype behind flags w/ counters first.
notes: Ablation pass for modes/knobs 8-11 completed. Summary: mode10 bounded path lookahead had the best residual graph (N=32000 bb=64 cols/rows mu 195.60 with k8/b2/d2 vs default 215.38), but current implementation is too slow for runtime use. Mode3/mode9 reduce residuals modestly to ~211.8; topK8 restores much of mode3 runtime at a small residual cost. Mode8 exact-W2 equals default. Mode5 seed sweep and high defer-percent hybrids were not promising. Exact incremental mode3 remains open in wirehair-n34.8; cheap path lookahead follow-up is wirehair-n34.18.

title: S4: Multi-destination GFNI muladd at heavy-solve / back-sub call sites
description: When one source block feeds several dest rows with different coeffs, load src once, compute several affine products. Targets heavy-row elimination & back-substitution. (codex P2)
notes:
Related GFNI task: check whether call-site batching is worthwhile after kernel/deeper-unroll measurement. Expect low end-to-end leverage unless heavy-solve sites show measurable time.
After WH_GFNI_UNROLL4 measurement, multi-destination GFNI remains low priority: GF multiply traffic is tiny in WH_COUNT, and even kernel-level GFNI improvements have mixed end-to-end impact. A call-site batching prototype should be limited to heavy-row/backsub hot spots and gated.

title: G1: SIMD-vectorize GE bit-matrix row XOR loops
description: Replace scalar uint64 ^= loops in PeelDiagonal/MultiplyDenseRows/TriangleNonHeavy/Triangle with AVX2/AVX-512 row-tail XOR. Pad _ge_pitch alignment, audit tail masking. Speeds encoder_create/decode for large N. (codex P5)

comments.author: catid
comments.text: CLOSED-WONTDO: profiling N=16000 shows GE bit-matrix ops (MultiplyDenseRows 0.68%, Triangle <0.6%) are negligible; 56% of time is data-block XOR (memory-bound). SIMD-ing GE bit XOR can't move end-to-end. Confirms memory-bound conclusion [[wirehair-memory-bound]].

title: S3: GFNI muladd multiple accumulators / deeper unroll
description: After S1, unroll GFNI muladd to 4x ZMM with independent accumulators to hide affine+xor latency and saturate ports.
notes:
Starting GFNI deeper-unroll/multi-accumulator experiment. Must measure end-to-end because previous count data shows GF multiply traffic is tiny versus XOR/memory traffic.
Implemented gated WH_GFNI_UNROLL4 prototype for gf256_mul_mem and gf256_muladd_mem. Micro selfcheck passed. Micro: mul_mem improved at 1024/16384/1MiB, muladd mixed. Fuzz passed: 57,472 trials, 0 fails. End-to-end GFNI matrix mixed: some create/decode improvements (e.g. N=2048 bb=1300 decode 2653->2777 MB/s, N=32000 bb=8192 decode 737->756), but recover regressed in several cells. Keep opt-in, not default.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

title: P5: Schur-complement separator experiment
description: Evaluate the Schur-complement / mix-heavy separator idea from the solver next-step list. First measure residual graph component sizes and separator coupling after compression; only prototype local-component elimination if histograms show useful separation. Validation: keep wire format unchanged, compare dense/deferred component histograms, symbol-rowop estimates, correctness fuzz, and wall time across block sizes 1280 bytes, 100 KiB, and 1 MiB before considering any production change.

title: O2: Heavy row count/width sweep (kHeavyRows/kHeavyCols)
description: Sweep kHeavyRows in {4,5,6,8} and heavy cols around 18. GFNI lowers GF256 cost so larger heavy tail may cut overhead. Changes code matrix => versioned. Measure mean & p99/p999 overhead, NeedMore rate, decode cycles. (codex P8)
notes:
Starting compile-time heavy matrix sweep support. Add WH_HEAVY_ROWS/WH_HEAVY_COLS overrides so variants can be measured without editing source constants.
Added WH_HEAVY_ROWS/WH_HEAVY_COLS compile-time overrides for safe subset sweeps (rows 1..6, cols rows..18); kHeavyMatrix remains the generated 6x18 table. Fuzz results: WH_HEAVY_ROWS=4 failed (4004 failures/73440 trials), WH_HEAVY_ROWS=5 failed (3730 failures/71424 trials), default 6x18 passed (68576 trials, 0 fails). Conclusion: current seed/dense/heavy tables are coupled to 6 rows; fewer rows are invalid without table regeneration, and >6 requires regenerating kHeavyMatrix and all seeds.
close_reason: Added sweep support and measured 4/5-row variants invalid without regeneration; default unchanged.

title: G4: Block-data row-op tiling for large block_bytes
description: Log block-data row-op schedule during solve, replay in 32-256KiB tiles to keep pivot/dest slices hot. Wins for large block_bytes, neutral/negative for MTU. (codex P4)
notes: Started 2026-06-06 with a standalone row-op tiling benchmark under experiments/tiling to avoid mixing with existing dirty codec changes. The harness verifies that replaying the full row-op schedule independently for each byte tile matches untiled output, then benchmarks synthetic dst^=src schedules with source fanout 8 at block sizes 1280, 100 KiB, and 1 MiB. Results saved in experiments/tiling/results/rowop_tiling_1280_100k_1m.csv and SUMMARY.md. Best observed speedups: 1280-byte symbols 0.994x (neutral/slightly negative), 100 KiB 1.285x at 16 KiB tiles, 1 MiB 2.090x at 16 KiB tiles. Normal verify and ASan/UBSan verify passed. Keep issue open: this does not yet log/replay actual AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal/Substitute schedules from the codec, and production final-block padding still needs validation.

title: G5: Heavy-row muladd mul==1 fast path / reduce GF256 op frequency
description: Detect identity multipliers in Triangle/BackSubstitute heavy elimination and use XOR path; measure fraction of identity entries.
notes:
Starting small heavy-row identity-multiplier fast-path experiment: replace gf256_muladd_mem(..., code_value=1, ...) with gf256_add_mem at heavy-row call sites where safe.
Implemented payload heavy-row code_value==1 fast paths in AddSubdiagonalValues and BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal, replacing gf256_muladd_mem with gf256_add_mem. Fuzz passed: 57,984 trials, 0 fails. WH_COUNT at N=32000 bb=64/1300 is effectively unchanged because GFmul traffic is already tiny (~0.01/0.14 MB vs hundreds MB XOR). End-to-end movement is mixed/noise; keep direct path as a small safe cleanup, not a major speed win.
close_reason: Implemented safe identity-multiplier fast path and measured neutral/mixed impact.

title: O1: Dense count re-optimization (speed-weighted objective)
description: Re-run GenerateDenseCount with objective mean_extra + lambda*decode_cycles, p999 constraint. Try D +/- 4 around current. Millions of trials at table knots + interpolation gaps. (codex P9)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: catid
comments.text: O1 measured: dense_count is a WEAK speed lever -- N=4096 reducing 74->50 (-32%) cuts create XOR amplification only 14.1x->13.8x (~2%), and raises overhead. MultiplyDenseValues is only 2.5x of 14x. The dominant 9.2x (Substitute 5.5x + PeelDiagonal 3.7x) is the peeling, set by the degree distribution -> needs full offline table regen (tradeoff). Closing O1 as low-value.

title: G2: M4RI / four-Russians for dense binary GE
description: Process k=4..8 pivot cols at once; build 2^k XOR-combination table, eliminate each row's k-bit pattern with one table XOR. Gate on _pivot_count/_ge_pitch thresholds. Expect 2-4x GE for large deferred systems. (codex P7)
notes: Related to wirehair-3ov pass: dense bit-matrix M4RI is lower priority than RHS symbol windowing because WH_COUNT shows symbol XOR traffic dominates.

title: G3: vpopcntdq-guided pivot selection (min tail weight)
description: Bounded-window pivot search choosing row with min remaining tail popcount via VPOPCNTDQ to reduce fill-in XORs and downstream heavy ops. (codex P6)

title: S5: Nontemporal stores + prefetch thresholds (mul_mem/addset_mem/output)
description: NT stores only where dest fully overwritten & not reread soon; prefetch large streams. Size-thresholded. (codex P12)

title: O4: Seed search upgrade (peel/dense, sequential-ID aware)
description: Expand seed search; optimize for sequential repair IDs as well as random. Better per-N/bucket seeds. A/B with equal Monte Carlo budget. (codex P11)
close_reason: Delivered reactive seed-search tooling and default-on peel/dense fixup path. Current validated result: [2048,32000] fuzz 0 failures, mean overhead near documented floor with known rare-tail/high-N residuals split to wirehair-0yk; long-term full table regeneration remains wirehair-jwg.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: FOUND concrete table defect: N=3061 default peel seed gives mean overhead 0.575 (p999=63, max=492) vs typical 0.02. Almost ANY other p_seed fixes it (pseed=5 -> 0.0155), a ~30x reduction. Peel-seed siblings (N%2048==1013: 1013,5109,7157,9205,11253) are fine, so it's a specific (pseed,dense_count,dseed) interaction at N=3061. Scanning full N range for all such weak spots, then exception-table fix.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: RESUMED post-reboot under safe limits (<=96 threads, single campaign, bb=64 low-bandwidth). Fixed a harness bug: scan/seedsearch/repro ignored --threads (dispatched before arg parse) -> ran at default ~102; stacking two = ~204 threads, which contributed to the hardware reset (marginal DIMM P0MH0). Added hard thread cap (hw-16, max 104). Validated seedsearch: weak N fixed (2962:1.55->0.02, 3430:0.77->0.02), good N unchanged (no overfit). Running full scan [2048,24000] thresh 0.10 to enumerate severe weak N, then seedsearch -> per-N peel-seed correction table in GetPeelSeed.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Scan [2048,24000] complete: 581 weak-spot N (~2.6% of range), 301 with mean overhead >1.0, 426 >0.3 (vs documented 0.02). Defect confirmed real & block-size-independent (N=3430: 0.527 at bb=64 == 0.547 at bb=1300). Nature: bimodal -- most decodes fine (p999<=1) but ~0.1-0.5% of erasure patterns catastrophic (up to 816 extra blocks). Each is a peel-seed defect: any non-default seed fixes it (verified at loss 0.10 AND 0.30). Full parallel seedsearch campaign running to build kPeelSeedFixups[] table. Harness bug fixed mid-stream: driver wait-loop pgrep matched my own status commands (string 'whx.base scan' in echo) -> never fired; now running seedsearch directly.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: FINAL overhead fix (peel 574 + dense 62 corrections, [2373,25787]): HEADLINE before/after fuzz [2048,32000] same seed: mean overhead 0.0893->0.0340 (-62%), max 2907->258 (-89%), 0 correctness fails. Refinement (joint peel+dense, goodthr=0.04, best-per-N generator) cut post-Task5 fuzz mean 0.077->0.025, max 1633->134. Residual: unfixed [25787,32000] hard N + 1 stubborn marginal (N=24146, best seeds have rare tails). Tables default-on, bug-safe (GetPeelSeed+GetDenseSeed binary-searched corrections).

comments.author: catid
comments.text: FINAL (extended to [2373,28033]): 606 peel + 92 dense corrections. Before/after fuzz [2048,32000] same seed (0 fails both): mean overhead 0.0893->0.0230 (-74%), max 2907->125 (-96%). Near the documented ~0.02. Default-on, bug-safe (GetPeelSeed+GetDenseSeed binary-searched). Remaining: [28033,32000] partial (cut for time) + [32000,64000] (same scan->joint->regen method). Pipeline: bench/whx scan+seedsearch[joint dense]+gen_fixups2.

title: O3: Peel degree distribution retune (weight-1 prob, soliton)
description: Optimize sparse degree distribution for lower deferred count + overhead, not just overhead. Adaptive P(weight=1) by N. Requires seed retune. (codex P10)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: catid
comments.text: O3/task6 QUANTIFIED the degree-distribution speed lever via a WH_PEELCAP knob + 6a counters. Capping peel-row weight at ~26-30 cuts create XOR amplification 14.1x->13.0x (~8%) and gives MEASURED ~5% faster create AND decode wall-time at N=4096, for +0.005 overhead (0.016->0.021, untuned seeds). BUT the naive global cap BREAKS encoder invertibility for some (N>=~7000, seed) combos (fuzz cap=30: 6999/267k encoder_create=null) -- conflicts with wirehair's seed-tuned invertibility guarantee. A safe cap (no failures to N=64000) is high (~56, ~2% speed). => Real ~5-8% speed lever but REQUIRES full offline table regeneration (re-tune all seeds/dense-counts/heavy for the capped distribution). This is THE concrete speed path; tracked as wirehair-31h (full regen). Knob gated behind WH_SEED_KNOBS, off by default.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: DEFINITIVE: peel-cap speed lever is NOT viable. Feasibility test (seedsearch under cap=30): large N (16000,32000) have NO working peel seed (best=1e9, encoder fails for all seeds); N=7301/10000 best stays 4.3/10.5. Lighter peeling overwhelms the fixed dense+heavy precode -> must GROW precode to invert -> adds back the traffic the cap saved. Degree distribution is deeply coupled to precode; the 13x is near-optimal. NO table-level speed win exists; a faster codec needs a different construction. Closing speed exploration.
